channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Mark-led ADC petitions INEC over rival faction’s nomination claim
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The David Mark-led ADC faction has petitioned INEC, alleging forgery and unauthorized use of documents by a rival group claiming to have uploaded nominees.
Read More: https://punchng.com/mark-led-adc-petitions-inec-over-rival-factions-nomination-claim/

item.title: Defence ministry blames funding gap for delayed interns’ allowances
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The Ministry of Defence clarifies that delayed interns’ allowances are due to a 2026 budgetary funding gap, not administrative failure. Payments have comme
Read More: https://punchng.com/defence-ministry-blames-funding-gap-for-delayed-interns-allowances/

item.title: Faldo: I'd say it to his face - DeChambeau has zero clue of Open strategy
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Sir Nick Faldo believes Bryson DeChambeau has "zero clue of strategy" when it comes to playing links golf.

item.title: A’Court upholds death sentence for Kwara cleric over lover’s murder
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Court of Appeal upholds the death sentence for Abdulrahman Bello, a Kwara cleric, convicted of murdering his lover, Hafsoh Yetunde Lawal. Read more on this
Read More: https://punchng.com/acourt-upholds-death-sentence-for-kwara-cleric-over-lovers-murder/

item.title: Nigerians demand NFF overhaul after Eagles’ W’Cup absence
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Following the Super Eagles’ second consecutive World Cup absence, Nigerians are demanding an NFF overhaul and accountability from the football federation’s
Read More: https://punchng.com/nigerians-demand-nff-overhaul-after-eagles-wcup-absence/

item.title: Oyo abduction: How kidnappers beat children, chained teachers – Principal Alamu
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The principal of Community Grammar School, Ahoro-Esiele, Mrs Racheal Alamu, has revealed how children abducted alongside teachers in Oriire Local Government Area of Oyo State were brutally beaten into silence by their captors, while male teachers were blindfolded, handcuffed and chained throughout t

item.title: World Cup: He reminds me of Zidane – John Terry names England player dragging team
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Chelsea legend, John Terry, has identified Jude Bellingham as the England player dragging the Three Lions through at the moment. According to Terry, Bellingham reminds him of former France midfielder Zinedine Zidane. The former England defender spoke to FIFA podcast ahead of England’s World Cu

item.title: Nigerian goalkeeper, Stanley Nwabali to challenge for position in Chippa United, Super Eagles
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Stanley Nwabali returned to Chippa United after he terminated his contract with the club by mutual consent in February this year. The Nigerian goalkeeper had requested to leave Chippa United to seek a new challenge elsewhere. Many expected Nwabali to move on to a bigger club after his impressive dis

item.title: Kogi Equips Facilitators With Skills To Curb Out-of-School Menace
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Kogi State Adolescent Girls Initiative for Learning and Empowerment (AGILE) Project has trained facilitators in 65 mass literacy education centres as part of the efforts to strengthen alternative education for out-of-school adolescent girls in the state. The three-day life skills training, organised

item.title: Children were beaten, men chained — Rescued Oyo principal recounts kidnappers’ brutality
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: "The youngest of them, Waliya and Salam, received the worst beating. They would cover their mouths with clothes, tie them and beat them severely," the principal said. The post Children were beaten, men chained — Rescued Oyo principal recounts kidnappers’ brutality appeared first on Premium Tim

item.title: Volkswagen confirms plans to slash up to 100,000 jobs globally
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The company also announced plans to reduce its vehicle lineup by... The post Volkswagen confirms plans to slash up to 100,000 jobs globally appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: Seadogs Call For ‘Moral Uprising’ Against Security Challenges
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The National Association of Seadogs (NAS), (Sahara Deck) has called for a “moral uprising” against Nigeria’s deepening security crisis, declaring that the real enemy is not just bandits or terrorists but a culture of silence, impunity and ethical collapse. The call was made in Abuj

item.title: World Cup 2026: Paul Scholes names biggest advantage Argentina have over England
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Manchester United legend, Paul Scholes, has named the ‘biggest advantage’ Argentina have over England ahead of both sides’ World Cup semi-final fixture on Wednesday. Both Argentina and England qualified for the World Cup last-4 after beating Switzerland and Norway in the quart

item.title: Insecurity: Gun battle erupts as bandits, security forces clash in Kebbi farming community
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Security forces came under attack on Monday while protecting farmers in Tudun Bichi community, Danko-Wasagu Local Government Area of Kebbi State, resulting in a gun battle with suspected bandits. The development was contained in a post shared by crisis reporter in the North-West region, Ba

item.title: Benue: Our Members Are Not Behind Otukpo Killings – MACBAN
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Benue State chapter of the Miyetti Allah Cattle Breeders Association of Nigeria (MACBAN) has exonerated its members from the recent killings of ten residents in Otukpo-Nobi, Otukpo Local Government Area of the State. MACBAN, while condemning the killings, urged the public not to attribute respon

item.title: 20 years behind bars for man who hid information on notorious bandit
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: The judge committed him to a 20-year jail term each in counts one and two without an option of fine.

item.title: Kebbi Enrols 50,000 Vulnerable Residents In Insurance Scheme
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Kebbi State government has approved the enrolment of 50,000 vulnerable people into its Social Health Insurance Scheme to expand access to quality healthcare and advancing Universal Health Coverage (UHC). The executive secretary of the Kebbi State Contributory Health Care Management Agency (KECHEMA)

item.title: Trump signals possible strike on Iran’s Pickaxe mountain nuclear facility
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: United States President Donald Trump has indicated that his administration is considering a military strike on Iran’s Pickaxe Mountain nuclear facility, suggesting the site could be targeted in the near future. Trump made the statement while speaking during an interview with conservative radio

item.title: Kinanee: Audit Of Correctional Centres Overdue
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The story of Gospel Kinanee, who spent 18 years in detention without trial after disappearing as a 14-year-old boy, is one of the most damning indictments of Nigeria’s criminal justice system in recent times. It is difficult to imagine how a child could enter the custody of the state and emer

item.title: Oyo: Terrorists Killed Michael, Deacon to Force Govt to a Deal, Oriire Principal Says
item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: • Relives harrowing experience of how they walked four hours in the bush   • Makinde assures pupils, teachers full support as military hands over victims • NHRC demands renewed push

item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: • ADC: Judgement didn’t affect our primaries  •Propaganda can’t change the law, says Atiku  •Party dismisses rival portal claim Chuks Okocha and Alex Enumah in Abuja The Court of Appeal,

item.title: Danny Glover breaks silence on battle with Alzheimer’s
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: The 80-year-old actor also explained that he has not fully accepted the diagnosis, stating that he has moments he can never forget.

item.title: Trump vows fresh strikes on Iran, backs Netanyahu amid escalating Gulf tensions
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: ​United States President Donald Trump has vowed to launch fresh military strikes against Iran, warning that Tehran would face overwhelming force as tensions over the Strait of Hormuz continue to deepen. Speaking during an interview with conservative radio host Hugh Hewitt, Trump declared that the Un

item.title: What to Expect From Wall Street Banks Earnings
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Analysts expect JPMorgan Chase & Co., Bank of America, Citigroup, Goldman Sachs Group and Morgan Stanley to report nearly $39 billion in trading revenue for the second quarter. Bloomberg's Balázs Penz breaks down the situation. (Source: Bloomberg)

item.title: Lindsey Graham’s sister appointed to complete late senator’s remaining senate term
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: South Carolina Governor, Henry McMaster has appointed Darline Graham Nordone, the sister of the late Senator Lindsey Graham, to temporarily fill the U.S. Senate seat left vacant following her brother’s death. McMaster announced the appointment during a news conference at the South Carolina Sta

item.title: From THISWEEK to THISDAY to ARISE News Channel, and Lekeelekee Social Media Platform: A Bold Generational Statement in Journalism Commemorates Founding
item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: •Sonala Olumhense pens tribute to THISWEEK: We can do it Forty years after THISWEEK first appeared on Nigerian newsstands, its founding editor, Sonala Olumhense, has offered a moving reflection on

item.title: Expert Advocates Robotics To Protect Infrastructure
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Regional director for West Africa of the Robotics and Autonomous Systems (RAS) Technical Section of the Society of Petroleum Engineers International (SPE International), Prof. Chinedu Ogwus, has called for the accelerated adoption of robotics and autonomous systems to safeguard Nigeria’s oil i

item.title: Troops Bust Arms Trafficking Ring, Recover Weapons In Kaduna
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Troops of Sector 7 of “Operation Enduring Peace” have disrupted a suspected arms trafficking syndicate and recovered a cache of illegal weapons during a coordinated, intelligence-driven operation in Kaduna State. A statement by the Nigerian Army on its official social media handle said t

item.title: Time for Lamine Yamal to ignite? Or another Mbappe World Cup masterclass?
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: France face Spain for a place in the World Cup final on Tuesday, meaning a showdown between Kylian Mbappe and Lamine Yamal. Who comes out on top?

item.title: Nasarawa judiciary begins annual vacation July 20, appoints two vacation judges
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Judges of the Nasarawa State High Court, along with Kadis of the Sharia Court of Appeal and Judges of the Customary Court of Appeal, will commence their 2026 annual vacation on Monday, July 20. The announcement was contained in a statement issued on Monday by the Covering Director of Information and

item.title: Plateau attack: Troops nab four suspects
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Troops of Sector 6, Operation Enduring Peace have arrested four suspects during a manhunt for those linked to the recent attack on Kumm Village in Riyom Local Government Area of Plateau State. The development was disclosed in a report by Zagazola Makama, a counter-insurgency and security analyst cov

item.title: Man Demands N530,000 From His Divorce-seeking Wife
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: A woman, Aisha Yunusa, has dragged her husband, Idris Yaqub, to a Shari’a Court at Magajin Gari, Kaduna, seeking divorce. The complainant, through her lawyer, Abubakar Ishaq, said she was no longer in love with her husband and could not remain in the marriage. She prayed the court to dissolve the un

item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: • Nigeria not a laboratory for reckless policy experiments, Atiku blasts Tinubu Chuks Okocha and Kuni Tyessi in Abuja The federal government has suspended the proposed review of registration fees

item.description: Yinka Kolawole in Osogbo For allegedly aiding and abetting political violence in Osun State, Governor Ademola Adeleke, has advocated economic sanctions and visa ban on Blue Marine Minister, Gboyega Oyetola,

Prince Ademola Adeniyi, purported Director-General of Director-General, DG of the controversial Presidential Foreign Intervention Promotion Council, PFIPC, says he does not have confidence in the probe of the Independent Corrupt Prac

item.title: Alleged Forgery: Ex-Minister of Science and Technology, Nnaji Arraigned, Denies Charges
item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: Alex Enumah in Abuja Former Minister of Innovation, Science and Technology, Mr. Uche Nnaji, was on Monday arraigned on a six-count charge bordering on alleged money laundering, corruption, forgery and

item.title: Kunav Appointees Endorse Alia for Second Term, Hail Infrastructure, Education Reforms
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The League of Kunav Appointees has endorsed Benue State governor, Rev. Fr. Hyacinth Alia, for a second term in office. The appointees passed a vote of confidence in his administration and commended what they described as remarkable achievements in infrastructure, education, industrialisation, worker

item.title: Education Minister to Lead Discussions as ECAN Marks Tinubu’s Three Years of Education Reforms
item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: Kuni Tyessi in Abuja The Minister of Education, Dr. Tunji Alausa, is billed to lead discussions on the achievements, reforms and challenges confronting Nigeria’s education sector under the administration of

item.title: Mark Condemns Benue, Plateau Killings, Demands Decisive Action Against Criminals
item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: • HURIWA, IPCR condemn devt, say govs must take responsibility Chuks Okocha and Michael Olugbode in Abuja The National Chairman of the African Democratic Congress (ADC), Senator David Mark, has

item.title: Uganda: Besigye Says He Was Brought to Court 'Under Duress'
item.dc:creator: AllAfrica
item.description: [Nile Post] Besigye tells court he was brought under duress and appeared without legal representation. Prosecution says repeated absence of defence lawyers is delaying the trial and seeks to proceed. Defence insists pending applications on legal representation and disclosure of evidence must be reso

item.title: Nnaji Pleads Not Guilty To Certificate Forgery Charges
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Former Minister of Science and Technology, Uche Nnaji, has been arraigned before Justice Joyce Abdulmalik of the Federal High Court in Abuja on charges of certificate forgery. He was granted bail in the sum of N20 million. Nnaji pleaded not guilty to six certificate forgery charges filed by the Inde

item.title: How US commerce secretary's Epstein links were uncovered by British whistleblower
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: Simon Andriesz made the discovery about Howard Lutnick in publicly released Epstein files.

item.title: UK’s Miliband Seen as Least Appealing Chancellor: Markets Pulse
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Andy Burnham has yet to announce his choice for UK Chancellor of the Exchequer, but among the presumptive next prime minister’s leading candidates, Energy Secretary Ed Miliband is seen as the least market-friendly choice, according to the latest Bloomberg Markets Live Pulse survey.

item.title: Zimbabwe: Matapi Makeover Wins Parliamentary Praise During Oversight Tour
item.dc:creator: AllAfrica
item.description: [263Chat] The Parliamentary Portfolio Committee on Local Government and National Housing has praised the ongoing refurbishment of the historic Matapi Flats in Mbare describing the project as a model for urban renewal and an example of how public-private partnerships can help address the country's ho

item.title: Kenya: 20 Arrested As Murkomen Orders Nationwide Crackdown On Political Violence
item.dc:creator: AllAfrica
item.description: [Capital FM] Nairobi -- Interior Cabinet Secretary Kipchumba Murkomen has confirmed the arrest of 20 people following weekend violence in Kisumu and Nyahururu, and ordered a nationwide intelligence-led operation to dismantle organized criminal gangs and arrest their financiers.

item.title: Gambia: 131 Gambians Died On Irregular Migration Routes in 2026, Ecowas Parliament Told
item.dc:creator: AllAfrica
item.description: [Foroyaa] A total of 131 Gambians have died while attempting irregular migration journeys in 2026, with the vast majority losing their lives at sea, Baye Gassama, Chief Executive Officer of the Network of Reintegration and Irregular Migration Data (NRIMD), has told members of the ECOWAS Parliament.

item.title: The electoral Act 2026 and the prospect for political stability (2)
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: a. OUSTER OF COURT’S JURISDICTION IN INTERNAL AFFAIRS OF POLITICAL PARTIES The explicit statutory ouster of jurisdiction in section 83 (5) of the 2026 Act is unprecedented, being a constitutional aberration. The foundation of the exercise of all legislative powers is the 1999 Constitution and anythi

item.title: China’s Green-Tech Exports Surge on Energy Transition Demand
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: China’s exports of green-tech products rose by more than a third in the first half of the year, driven by the accelerating global energy transition.

item.title: Indonesia Says S&P Affirmation to Help Stabilize Bond Market
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Indonesia expects S&P Global Ratings’ vote of confidence in its sovereign credit profile to help stabilize the domestic bond market after a selloff in June, according to a top finance ministry official.

item.title: U.S. Soy Ready to Help Bridge Nigeria’s Protein Gap
item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: As Nigeria grapples with rising food costs and a widening animal-protein gap, U.S. soybean leaders say the solution lies not in competing with local farmers but in collaboration, improved technology, and workforce training. In separate interviews during the USSEC & U.S. Soy Nigeria: Now Conferen

item.title: China Expels Former Politburo Member Over Graft and Sex Charges
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: China expelled former Politburo member Ma Xingrui from the ruling Communist Party, accusing him of a wide range of offenses including corruption, abuse of power and trading political favors for sex.

item.title: UAE condemns Iran's 'brazen' attack on tankers as US launches fresh strikes
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: The third consecutive night of US strikes on Iran came as Trump announced a 20% charge as part of a new blockade on the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: South Korean Won Climbs to Two-Month High on Hynix ADR Inflows
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: The South Korean won advanced to its strongest level since May on expectations that dollar inflows from SK Hynix Inc’s American depositary receipts will enter the local currency market.

item.title: INEC aiding Tinubu’s agenda to disenfranchise ADC, other opposition parties, Atiku alleges
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: •Your claim is false, only David Mark-led exco gets access codes —commission’s top official The presidential candidate of the African Democratic Congress (ADC) has accused the Independent National Electoral Commission (INEC) of partisanship and aiding what it described as President Bola Tinubu

item.title: Sudan: UN Raises Alarm Over a Sharp Rise in Human Rights Abuses and Cholera-Related Deaths in Sudan
item.dc:creator: AllAfrica
item.description: [IPS] United Nations -- Throughout 2026, the humanitarian crisis in Sudan has deteriorated significantly, prompting the United Nations (UN) to raise alarm over the escalation of human rights violations. Persistent clashes between the Sudanese Armed Forces (SAF) and the Rapid Support Forces (RSF) con

item.title: Palm Oil Advances as Crude Rally Boosts Outlook for Biofuels
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Palm oil climbed for a second day as a surge in crude prices fueled by renewed US-Iran hostilities brightened demand prospects for biofuels.

item.title: South Korea Plans FX Rule Easing to Expand Won’s Global Use
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: South Korea plans to relax foreign-exchange rules governing capital transactions in the second half of the year as part of a broader effort to internationalize the won and deepen overseas use of the currency.

item.title: World Cup 2026: Ahead of France-Spain, in Dallas, supporters are confident
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: Ahead of the World Cup semi-final between France and Spain on Tuesday, July 14, French supporters (and others) are feeling confident about the outcome of the match.

item.title: World Cup 2026: ‘We Can Beat Them’ –Rodri Speaks Ahead Spain Vs France
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: Spain captain Rodri has expressed confidence that the team can beat France in tonight’s FIFA World Cup 2026 semi-final matchup. Acknowledging France’s form and physical defensive structure, he believes past victories, such as at Euro 2024, prove Spain has the strategy and quality to over

item.description: In Nigeria, one of the most important things many Nigerians fail to understand about investing is that their strategy should read more How to invest according to your life stage

item.description: THE streets of Osogbo witnessed a disturbing spectacle on Monday, June 29 as students of the Osun State University (UNIOSUN) took to the streets in protest against the alleged invasion of privately-owned hostels in the Oke Baale area by soldiers, resulting in s3xual assault of female students, bruta

item.title: Birthday present for Spain’s Yamal? World Cup semifinal win over France
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Yamal wants to celebrate his 19th birthday by taking Spain to the World Cup final in New York.

item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Nigeria Union of Teachers (NUT) has called for the full implementation of the Safe Schools Initiative, deployment of adequate security personnel and surveillance systems to vulnerable schools, improved intelligence gathering and community-based security mechanisms The post NUT demands security beef

item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Constituents of the lawmakers representing Abia North Senatorial District, Dr. Orji Uzor Kalu, have expressed delight over the award of a two-kilometre Umuakpaeme Item road. item.title: Nigeria, Hong Kong sign double taxation treaty to boost trade, investment
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: The agreement is expected to eliminate double taxation on income earned in both jurisdictions, strengthen tax cooperation and encourage greater cross-border investment. item.title: World Cup 2026: France Won’t Allow Spain To Control Possession –Kounde
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: Barcelona star Jules Koundé has disclosed that France can’t afford to spend 90 minutes chasing the ball in tonight’s FIFA World Cup 2026 semi-final clash against Spain. This will be just the second ever World Cup meeting between France and Spain, following their last 16 meeting back in 2

item.title: Obi bemoans renewed killings in Benue, Plateau
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Mr Peter Obi, 2027 Presidential Candidate of the National Democratic Congress (NDC), yesterday decried the renewed heartless killings in Benue and Plateau. The post Obi bemoans renewed killings in Benue, Plateau appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Senator representing Abia North, Orji Uzor Kalu, has dismissed claims that President Bola Tinubu’s push for state police is driven by political considerations ahead of the 2027 general election, saying he motivation is the safety of Nigerians. item.title: 56 days with kidnappers: Oyo principal recounts ordeal
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The harrowing details of the victims’ 56-day captivity emerged yesterday as the rescued pupils and teachers were formally handed over to the Oyo State Government by the military. The post 56 days with kidnappers: Oyo principal recounts ordeal appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Mexico to formally ask prosecutors to file cases in US courts over its citizens' deaths in immigration operations.

item.description: French coach Deschamps says his team will be fully prepared for a midfield battle against the European champions.

item.title: France, Nigeria leverage €27bn commitment to transform African agricultural value chains
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: France and Nigeria are set to deepen agricultural cooperation by leveraging a broader €27 billion commitment announced during the Africa Forward Summit in Nairobi to accelerate financing for African agricultural value chains and boost agri-food trade. The initiative, which includes the Food and Agri

item.title: Death toll from Bangkok bar fire rises to 30 as police probe safety lapses
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: Survivors and first reporters reported doors being locked and the lack of signage marking emergency exits.

item.title: Japan Bond Sale Draws High Demand After Katayama’s GPIF Remarks
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Japan’s 20-year government bond auction Tuesday attracted strong investor demand after remarks by Finance Minister Satsuki Katayama boosted sentiment toward sovereign debt.

item.title: Oil prices hit 1-month high as US-Iran attacks dim Strait of Hormuz outlook
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Brent crude nears $85 a barrel amid renewed hostiles between Washington and Tehran.

item.title: Agric ministry moves to strengthen partnerships with NIMC over farmers’ identification
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The Federal Ministry of Agriculture and Food Security (FMAFS) has reiterated its readiness to strengthen collaboration and robust partnership with National Identity Management Commission (NIMC) to ensure digital identity infrastructure and the National Identification Number (NIN) and build a verifia

item.title: The boiling waters of Bille
item.dc:creator: Guardian NG Opinion
item.description: By Hope O’Rukevbe Eghagha A social media video of a community with boiling waters is the immediate trigger of this intervention in the form of an essay. At first, I thought it was an AI generated image like the video The post The boiling waters of Bille appeared first on The Guardian Nigeria News -

item.title: #NBADecides2026: What future for an independent bar?
item.dc:creator: Guardian NG Opinion
item.description: Chidi Anselm Odinkalu Three score and three years ago, a defining dispute erupted in the old Western Region of Nigeria over the scope and reach of constitutional conventions in determining or terminating the tenure of high-level political leadership. The Judicial The post #NBADecides2026: What futur

item.title: Collateral consequences of President Tinubu’s healthcare reforms
item.dc:creator: Guardian NG Opinion
item.description: By Okey Akpa Few aspects of President Bola Ahmed Tinubu’s reform programme have attracted more public attention than the economy. Debate has centred on fiscal policy, exchange-rate reforms, inflation, taxation, investment and the cost of living. These are the measures The post Collateral consequence

item.title: Insecurity: The possible way out
item.dc:creator: Guardian NG Opinion
item.description: By Eric Teniola If we assume that kidnapping, insurgency and other security problems are politically motivated, then the solution is political not military and we must tackle these challenges through dialogue. As Sir Winston Churchill, the former British Prime Minister once said, The post Insecurity

item.title: Cup of lessons from the World Cup 2026
item.dc:creator: Guardian NG Opinion
item.description: By Anthony Akinwale It is extremely rare that one’s country play in the World Cup in the city in which one lives twice in one’s lifetime. The host nation changes every four years, and chances of one’s country qualifying for The post Cup of lessons from the World Cup 2026 appeared first on The Guardi

item.title: Yoruba Times: Medium for the people, voice for a nation
item.dc:creator: Guardian NG Opinion
item.description: By Dr Adebukola Adeniji Sir: When we set out to establish The Yoruba Times, we did not set out to start a newspaper. We set out to build a media institution dedicated to a mission: to defend the truth, to The post Yoruba Times: Medium for the people, voice for a nation appeared first on The Guardian

item.title: The PFIPC scandal and burden of public trust
item.dc:creator: Guardian NG Opinion
item.description: Among other concerns, the unfolding public scandal over the operation of the Presidential Foreign Intervention Promotion Council (PFIPC) raises critical questions about institutional accountability, public financial controls and how a purportedly fictitious agency could allegedly obtain official rec

item.title: Cartoon
item.dc:creator: Guardian NG Opinion
item.description: By Osas. A The post Cartoon appeared first on The Guardian Nigeria News - Nigeria and World News.

item.title: ICE officer kills motorist in Maine; the second fatality in a week
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: An Immigration and Customs Enforcement officer has fatally shot a motorist in Maine.
